sources.notes: Statement and full binary tree expression constraints.
Official editorial confirms comparing extremely fast-growing expressions by their asymptotic exponent structure.
Independent Chinese writeup used to cross-check the persistent segment tree/hash comparison approach.

annotation.summary: Compare or order values represented by recursively defined huge expressions on a tree.
annotation.constraints: Values are far too large to compute directly, so comparison must use structural/asymptotic signatures and efficient merging.
annotation.core_idea: Represent each subtree expression by a canonical comparison signature describing its leading tower/exponent contribution. Merge child signatures bottom-up and compare them lexicographically by asymptotic dominance rather than numeric value. Persistent segment trees or hashed sparse vectors support fast merge and comparison of these signatures.
annotation.complexity: Approximately O(n log n) with persistent segment tree and hashing
tricks: Compare enormous expressions by asymptotic dominance instead of evaluating them.
Use hashes to accelerate equality and lexicographic comparison.

solution_variants.name: persistent-signature-comparison
solution_variants.summary: Build canonical asymptotic signatures for tree expressions and compare them with persistent segment tree plus hashing.

tags.evidence: The central operation is comparing expression growth by leading asymptotic structure instead of exact values.
tags.description: Compare quantities that are too large to evaluate by their asymptotic growth, leading term, exponent tower, or dominance signature.
tags.created_reason: Some high-difficulty problems require ordering gigantic symbolic values; generic math or hashing tags do not capture this comparison technique.

tags.evidence: Once signatures are built, their order is determined by the first differing dominant component.
The implementation stores and merges comparison signatures with persistent segment trees.
Hashes are used to quickly test equality and guide comparison of persistent signatures.
The huge expressions are generated and merged along a tree structure.
The persistent structure is a segment-tree based sparse signature representation.
